Journal.Title: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition

Article.ArticleTitle: Creatine monohydrate supplementation for older adults and clinical populations.

Abstract.AbstractText: The biological process of aging is typically associated with a decrease in muscle quantity, muscle performance (primarily strength), bone mass and architecture, functionality and neurological/cognitive function. From a healthy aging perspective, interventions that have the potential to overcome or attenuate these decrements are clinically relevant.
We conducted a narrative review on the efficacy of creatine monohydrate supplementation (CrM) in older adults.

Accumulating research shows that CrM, primarily when combined with exercise training, is safe and has beneficial effects on measures of whole-body lean body mass, regional muscle size, muscle strength, bone area and thickness, functional ability, glucose kinetics, cognition and memory.
CrM has multiple benefits in older adults and may have application for treating age-related sarcopenia, osteoporosis, frailty, and those with metabolic and neuromuscular disorders.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: DGC has received research grants and performed industry sponsored research involving creatine supplementation, received creatine donation for scientific studies and travel support for presentations involving creatine supplementation at scientific conferences. In addition, DGC serves on the Scientific Advisory Board for Alzchem and Create (companies which manufacture creatine) and the editorial review board for the Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition. DGC is also a sports science advisor to the International Society of Sports Nutrition and serves as an expert witness/consultant in legal cases involving creatine supplementation. SMO serves as a member of the Scientific Advisory Board on Creatine in Health and Medicine (AlzChem LLC). SMO co-owns patent “Supplements Based on Liquid Creatine” at the European Patent Office (WO2019150323 A1) and patent application “Composition Comprising Creatine for Use in Telomere Lengthening” at the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (# 63/608,850). SMO has received research support related to creatine during the past 36 months from the Serbian Ministry of Science, Technological Development and Innovation; Provincial Secretariat for Higher Education and Scientific Research; AlzChem GmbH; Kaneka Nutrients; ThermoLife International, and Vireo System Inc. SMO does not own stocks and shares in any organization. PDC serves on the Scientific Advisory Committee for Osteoporosis Canada. BG has received research grants, creatine donations for scientific studies, travel support for participation in scientific conferences, and an honorarium for speaking at lectures from AlzChem. In addition, B.G. serves on the Scientific Advisory Board for Alzchem. MAT is the CEO of Exerkine Corporation. The company products that have been shown to improve mitochondrial function and these products are commercially available for healthy aging and obesity (TRIM7 and MUSCLE5). These products contain protein, creatine monohydrate, coenzyme Q10, alpha lipoic acid, green tea, green coffee bean extract, caffeine and forskolin. MAT has received remuneration for speaker honoraria and advisory board duties from Sanofi-Genzyme in 2022–24 and Amicus Therapeutics in 2024–25. RBK has conducted industry-sponsored research on creatine, received financial support for presenting at conferences, and has served as an expert witness throughout his career. Additionally, he serves as Chair of the Scientific Advisory Board for AlzChem, GmHb (a company that makes creatine monohydrate), is a co-founder of the nonprofit International Society of Sports Nutrition (ISSN), and a member of the scientific advisory boards for Oath Nutrition and Trace Minerals. SCF has served as a scientific advisor for a company that sells creatine products and is a scientific advisor of Bear Balanced. SCF has received creatine donations from Creapure for research purposes. JA is the CEO and co-founder of the International Society of Sports Nutrition (ISSN), an academic nonprofit. The ISSN may be sponsored by companies that manufacture, market, and sell creatine-containing supplements, including Creapure, Bear Balanced, and Create. IL, TW, TM and US declare no competing interests.
